title: UN watchdog confirms Iran possesses enriched uranium for up to 10 nuclear weapons
summary: The IAEA reports Iran retains enough enriched uranium for up to 10 nuclear bombs if weaponized, while negotiations to decouple the nuclear program from war-ending talks proceed slowly.
contents:
# IAEA Confirms Iran's Enriched Uranium Stockpile Sufficient for 10 Weapons

## Key Finding
The International Atomic Energy Agency confirms Iran possesses enough enriched uranium for up to 10 nuclear weapons if Tehran decides to weaponize. This assessment emerged amid stalled US-Iran negotiations.

## Negotiation Status
Iran proposed separating its nuclear program from broader war-ending discussions and Strait of Hormuz reopening. The proposal represents a significant diplomatic shift, decoupling the nuclear file from ceasefire conditions.

## Policy Analysis
UANI Policy Director Jason Brodsky and geopolitical analysts note the stockpile growth occurred during the ongoing US-Iran conflict, raising questions about enforcement effectiveness. The enrichment program continued uninterrupted despite military operations.

## Implications
- Iran retains full ballistic missile capabilities
- Strait of Hormuz blockade remains in effect
- Nuclear talks proceed on a separate track from ceasefire negotiations
- Regional escalation risks remain elevated

## Tags
iran, nuclear-program, iaea, enriched-uranium, nonproliferation, sanctions
